briefing:
## 🌍 Top 5 Global Fintech News

- **[Goodbye Visa and Mastercard: 130M Europeans switching to sovereign payment](https://www.lesnumeriques.com/banque-en-ligne/adieu-visa-et-mastercard-130-millions-d-europeens-basculent-vers-un-paiement-100-souverain-des-2026-n250918.html)** — *Hacker News* — Over 130 million Europeans are set to migrate to a sovereign European payment network in 2026, signalling a major shift away from US card giants Visa and Mastercard.

- **[Fintech Engineering Handbook](https://w.pitula.me/fintech-engineering-handbook/)** — *Hacker News* — A comprehensive technical resource for engineers building fintech products, covering architecture, compliance, and payments infrastructure.

- **[Americans see their savings vanish in Synapse fintech crisis](https://www.cnbc.com/2024/11/22/synapse-bankruptcy-thousands-of-americans-see-their-savings-vanish.html)** — *Hacker News* — The collapse of Banking-as-a-Service middleware provider Synapse left thousands of Americans unable to access their deposits, exposing critical risks in fintech-bank partnership models.

- **[Fintech founder charged with fraud; AI app found to be humans in the Philippines](https://techcrunch.com/2025/04/10/fintech-founder-charged-with-fraud-after-ai-shopping-app-found-to-be-powered-by-humans-in-the-philippines/)** — *Hacker News* — A fintech founder faces fraud charges after their purportedly AI-powered shopping app was revealed to be operated by human workers in the Philippines.

- **[Open Banking and Payments Competition](https://www.bitsaboutmoney.com/archive/open-banking-and-payments-competition/)** — *Hacker News* — An in-depth analysis of how open banking frameworks are reshaping competition in the payments industry across major markets.

---

## 🍁 Top 5 Canadian Fintech News

- **[Feds release proposed open banking regulations](https://betakit.com/feds-release-proposed-open-banking-regulations/)** — *BetaKit* — The Canadian federal government has published its long-awaited proposed regulatory framework for open banking, marking a pivotal step toward consumer-directed finance in Canada.

- **[Conexus Venture Capital invests in FinTech Judi.ai](https://betakit.com/conexus-venture-capital-invests-in-fintech-judi-ai/)** — *BetaKit* — Credit union-backed Conexus Venture Capital has made a new investment in Canadian AI-powered fintech Judi.ai, signalling growing credit union interest in fintech innovation.

- **[RBC fined $4.25M over incorrect credit card statements](https://www.cbc.ca/news/business/rbc-fined-credit-card-statements-9.7249811?cmp=rss)** — *CBC Business* — Canada's largest bank has been fined $4.25 million by regulators after issuing inaccurate credit card statements to customers, raising fresh questions about consumer protection compliance.

- **[Canada is gaining corporate VCs, but still has "a participation problem," report finds](https://betakit.com/canada-is-gaining-corporate-vcs-but-still-has-a-participation-problem-report-finds/)** — *BetaKit* — A new report finds that while corporate venture capital activity is growing in Canada, a persistent gap in investor participation continues to challenge the broader startup ecosystem.

- **[Edmonton's digital Nostradamus, and the early-stage funding gap](https://betakit.com/edmontons-digital-nostradamus-and-the-early-stage-funding-gap/)** — *BetaKit* — A deep dive into Canada's ongoing early-stage funding gap highlights the structural challenges facing founders trying to raise pre-seed and seed capital outside major tech hubs.

---

## 🌊 Bonus: Relevant to Atlantic Fintech's Focus (1–3 articles)

- **[Canada calls screen scraping 'unsecure,' sets Open Banking target for 2023](https://finledger.com/2021/08/09/canada-sets-open-banking-target-for-2023/)** — *Hacker News* — Highly relevant context as Canada's open banking regulations are now finally being proposed in 2026 — a reminder of the long road Atlantic fintechs and credit unions have navigated to get here.

- **[Apple acquires UK open banking startup Credit Kudos](https://ffnews.com/newsarticle/apple-acquires-uk-open-banking-startup-credit-kudos/)** — *Hacker News* — Apple's acquisition of an open banking startup underscores how Big Tech is positioning itself within the open finance ecosystem, a dynamic that Atlantic Canadian fintechs and credit unions should monitor closely.

- **[Gander Social to launch to public on Canada Day](https://betakit.com/gander-social-to-launch-to-public-on-canada-day/)** — *BetaKit* — Gander Social — named after the Newfoundland city — is launching publicly on Canada Day, offering a noteworthy moment of Atlantic Canadian visibility in the national tech conversation.
